items.summary: Restore the full Version 24 UI exactly as it was, including the company logo, layout, color scheme, navigation, and all existing components (summary cards, bill table, add bill form, search, party summary, company report, settings, invoice print view). Do not alter any existing Version 24 design or functionality.

Add a party/buyer section to the backend Bill data model with three new fields: partyGstNumber (Text), partyAddress (Text). The party name is already stored; ensure partyGstNumber and partyAddress are persisted on each bill record. Update addBill and editBill backend functions to accept and store these fields. Ensure backward compatibility with existing bill records (default to empty strings if missing).

Add party/buyer input fields to the AddBillForm and EditBillModal components: Party GST Number (text input, auto-filled from the selected party's stored GST if available) and Party Address (text input or textarea, auto-filled from the selected party's stored address if available). These fields should appear in a clearly labeled 'Bill To / Party Details' section without disrupting the Version 24 layout of other fields.

Display the party/buyer's name, GST number, and address in the InvoicePrintView component. These should appear in a 'Bill To' section on the invoice, formatted clearly alongside the existing company/seller details. The GST number must be labeled (e.g., 'GST No:') and the address shown below the party name. This section must be visible both on screen and when printing.

Display the party/buyer's GST number in the invoice summary views: on the SummaryPage bills table and in the SearchBillsPage results table, add a visible column or inline detail showing the party GST number for each bill. Also ensure the PartySummaryPage shows the party GST number alongside party name.
